message.content:
You are an independent adversarial fix-confirmation reviewer (reviewer B) for hc-assay at /Users/[REDACTED]/hc-assay (branch harden/pass-9). Confirm the fix is appropriate AND complete (no residual gap; durable; tests discriminate). Read the files and grep the committed corpus yourself (git grep / grep -ri). Judge ONLY P9-PII-1.

P9-PII-1 (operator machine/namespace hostname leak) — ROUND 2 after a confirm-concern. The prior round CONFIRMED the machine hostname ([REDACTED], auto-derived) was scrubbed to 0, but found [REDACTED] was RE-INTRODUCED every commit because the pre-commit hook ran capture WITHOUT ASSAY_SCRUB_HOSTS, so _hostname_patterns() emitted no namespace rule. THE FIX NOW: .githooks/pre-commit exports ASSAY_SCRUB_HOSTS=hc-grc (honoring an external value) before invoking scripts/capture_transcripts.py; the corpus was re-scrubbed; _hostname_patterns() redacts the machine hostname bare+.local and an ASSAY_SCRUB_HOSTS host's .local form only (bare 'hc-grc' preserved). VERIFY on branch harden/pass-9: (1) grep transcripts/ for '[REDACTED]' AND '[REDACTED]' (case-insensitive) → expect 0 genuine leaks (a commit just re-captured the active session WITH the env, so it must stay 0); (2) bare 'hc-grc' still present (~1400, project token, not over-redacted); (3) the hook actually exports ASSAY_SCRUB_HOSTS before capture; (4) tests test_scrub_redacts_machine_and_namespace_hostnames + test_precommit_hook_wires_namespace_host_into_capture pass and discriminate. Is the operator-hostname PII vector now actually closed AND durable against future captures?

attachment.content:
- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled cloud agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring cloud agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Fable, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

content.content:
#!/usr/bin/env bash
# Provenance: snapshot the active Claude session transcript(s) into transcripts/ on every commit
# so each commit carries an auditable record of the agent interactions/effort behind it.
# Best-effort — never block a commit.
set -u
ROOT="$(git rev-parse --show-toplevel 2>/dev/null)" || exit 0
PY="$ROOT/.venv/bin/python"
[ -x "$PY" ] || PY="$(command -v python3 || true)"
[ -n "$PY" ] || exit 0
# Wire the internal namespace host(s) into the scrubber's capture path (#P9-PII-1). The machine
# hostname auto-derives (socket/scutil), but an RDF-namespace host like "[REDACTED]" embedded in
# project files only redacts when named here — otherwise every commit re-introduces it. We pass the
# bare label "hc-grc"; the scrubber redacts ONLY its ".local" form (the bare project token, which
# appears in legitimate paths/repo refs, is preserved). Honor an externally-set value if present.
export ASSAY_SCRUB_HOSTS="${ASSAY_SCRUB_HOSTS:-hc-grc}"
"$PY" "$ROOT/scripts/capture_transcripts.py" 2>/dev/null || true
git add -- "$ROOT/transcripts" 2>/dev/null || true
exit 0

content.text: The bare hc-grc count is ~152k, far higher than the "~1400" stated. That discrepancy is worth understanding — likely the prompt's ~1400 was distinct-line or distinct-file count, while raw occurrences are much higher. The key point is bare token is preserved (not over-redacted). Let me note this and verify the count interpretation, then inspect the scrubber implementation and tests.

content.content:
185	            # accidentally spell out the handle, and common single tokens (e.g. "CISO") are NOT
186	            # redacted because the WHOLE letter sequence (…[REDACTED]) must be present in order.
187	            pats.append(re.compile(r"[\s._-]*".join(re.escape(c) for c in h), re.IGNORECASE))
188	        else:
189	            pats.append(re.compile(re.escape(h), re.IGNORECASE))
190	    return pats
191	
192	
193	def _hostname_patterns() -> list[re.Pattern[str]]:
194	    """Redactions for the operator's machine/infrastructure hostname (#P9-MO/P9-PII-1).
195	
196	    The scrubber masked email/username/display-name/handle but had NO rule for the host the node
197	    runs on, so the production machine's hostname (e.g. ``[REDACTED]`` / ``[REDACTED]``) and
198	    internal ``*.local`` mDNS/namespace hosts leaked into committed transcripts — operator/infra
199	    identity. Derive the MACHINE hostname from ``socket.gethostname``/``platform.node`` (and, on
200	    macOS, ``scutil --get LocalHostName``/``ComputerName``) and redact both its bare and ``.local``
201	    forms (a machine name is distinctive). Additional internal hosts (e.g. an RDF-namespace host
202	    like ``[REDACTED]``) are supplied via the comma-separated ``ASSAY_SCRUB_HOSTS`` env and have
203	    ONLY their ``.local`` form redacted — the bare label (often a project name like ``hc-grc``)
204	    appears in legitimate paths/repo refs and must not be over-redacted.
205	    """
206	    machine: list[str] = []
207	    try:
208	        machine.append(socket.gethostname())
209	    except Exception:  # noqa: BLE001
210	        pass
211	    try:
212	        machine.append(platform.node())
213	    except Exception:  # noqa: BLE001
214	        pass
215	    try:
216	        import subprocess
217	
218	        for arg in ("LocalHostName", "ComputerName"):
219	            out = subprocess.run(["scutil", "--get", arg], capture_output=True, text=True, timeout=5)
220	            if out.returncode == 0 and out.stdout.strip():
221	                machine.append(out.stdout.strip())
222	    except Exception:  # noqa: BLE001 - scutil absent (non-macOS) must never break capture
223	        pass
224	    extra = [h.strip() for h in os.environ.get("ASSAY_SCRUB_HOSTS", "").split(",") if h.strip()]
225	
226	    pats: list[re.Pattern[str]] = []
227	    seen: set[str] = set()
228	
229	    def _base(name: str) -> str:
230	        n = name.strip()
231	        return n[: -len(".local")] if n.lower().endswith(".local") else n
232	
233	    for name in machine:  # machine hostname → redact BARE + .local (distinctive identity)
234	        b = _base(name)
235	        if len(b) < 4 or b.lower() in seen:
236	            continue
237	        seen.add(b.lower())
238	        pats.append(re.compile(re.escape(b) + r"(?:\.local)?\b", re.IGNORECASE))
239	    for name in extra:  # supplied namespace hosts → redact ONLY the .local FQDN form
240	        b = _base(name)
241	        if len(b) < 4 or ("local:" + b.lower()) in seen:
242	            continue
243	        seen.add("local:" + b.lower())
244	        pats.append(re.compile(re.escape(b) + r"\.local\b", re.IGNORECASE))
245	    return pats
246	
247	
248	_USERNAME_RE = _username_pattern()
249	_DISPLAY_NAME_RES = _display_name_patterns()
250	_REPO_HANDLE_RES = _repo_handle_patterns()
251	_HOSTNAME_RES = _hostname_patterns()
252	
253	
254	def _scrub(text: str) -> str:
255	    for pat in _SECRET_PATTERNS:
256	        if pat.groups == 2:
257	            text = pat.sub(r"\1[REDACTED]\2", text)
258	        elif pat.groups == 1:
259	            text = pat.sub(r"\1[REDACTED]", text)

content.text: Implementation matches the description. The machine host gets bare+`.local`, extra hosts get `.local` only. One thing to check: `_HOSTNAME_RES` is computed at module import time, so the env var must be set BEFORE the script runs (which the hook does via export). Let me read the tests and verify discrimination.

content.content:
199	def test_scrub_redacts_machine_and_namespace_hostnames(monkeypatch):
200	    # #P9-PII-1: the production node's hostname and internal *.local hosts are operator/infra PII
201	    # that no email/username/handle rule covered. The machine hostname is redacted bare + .local;
202	    # an ASSAY_SCRUB_HOSTS namespace host has its .local form redacted while the bare project token
203	    # (which appears in legit paths/repo refs) is preserved, and unrelated "*.local" is untouched.
204	    monkeypatch.setenv("ASSAY_SCRUB_HOSTS", "hc-proj")
205	    cap = _load("scripts/capture_transcripts.py", "capture_transcripts_host")
206	    # force a deterministic machine hostname regardless of the test host
207	    monkeypatch.setattr(cap.socket, "gethostname", lambda: "buildbox-7")
208	    monkeypatch.setattr(cap.platform, "node", lambda: "buildbox-7")
209	    cap._HOSTNAME_RES = cap._hostname_patterns()
210	    out = cap._scrub(
211	        "ran on buildbox-7.local (bare buildbox-7); ns https://hc-proj.local/prov#; "
212	        "repo hc-proj/app and settings.local.json"
213	    )
214	    assert "buildbox-7" not in out  # machine hostname redacted (bare + .local)
215	    assert "hc-proj.local" not in out  # namespace host .local form redacted
216	    assert "hc-proj/app" in out  # bare project token preserved (not over-redacted)
217	    assert "settings.local.json" in out  # unrelated *.local untouched (no blanket rule)
218	
219	
220	def test_precommit_hook_wires_namespace_host_into_capture():
221	    # #P9-PII-1 confirm-concern: the namespace host ([REDACTED]) only redacts when ASSAY_SCRUB_HOSTS
222	    # names it; the machine hostname auto-derives but this does not. The pre-commit hook must set
223	    # ASSAY_SCRUB_HOSTS before running capture, else every commit re-introduces [REDACTED].
224	    hook = (_ROOT / ".githooks" / "pre-commit").read_text(encoding="utf-8")
225	    assert "ASSAY_SCRUB_HOSTS" in hook, "pre-commit hook does not wire the namespace host scrub"
226	    # it must be exported BEFORE the capture invocation
227	    pre = hook.split("capture_transcripts.py")[0]
228	    assert "ASSAY_SCRUB_HOSTS" in pre, "ASSAY_SCRUB_HOSTS set after capture (too late)"
229	
230	
231	def test_example_uses_no_hardcoded_hmac_secret():
232	    # #F-048: the example must not carry a shippable, publicly-known HMAC secret a user could copy
233	    # into production. It derives a fresh random secret per run instead.
234	    src = (_ROOT / "examples" / "minimal_study.py").read_text(encoding="utf-8")
235	    assert b"example-study-secret-key".decode() not in src  # the stale weak literal is gone
236	    assert "os.urandom(32)" in src  # the fresh-per-run secret is in place
237

content.text: Both tests are well-constructed and discriminating. Let me run them and verify the corpus was actually re-captured with the env (the most recent commit re-captured the active session — confirm it stayed at 0). Let me also check that the latest commit's transcript additions contain no leak, and run the tests.

Let me confirm test discrimination by mutation: revert the hook fix and confirm the hook test fails; and verify the redaction actually depends on the `.local`-only rule by checking the scrubber would fail without it. Let me do a quick negative check on the hook test.

content.text:
This confirms the root-cause mechanism exactly: without `ASSAY_SCRUB_HOSTS` set, `hc-proj.local` (analog of `[REDACTED]`) leaks through; the scrubber test sets the env so it would catch a regression in the redaction rule. The hook is what guarantees the env is set at capture time.

Let me do a final durability check: confirm the hook is the actually-installed git hook (core.hooksPath) so future commits really run it, since a hook in `.githooks/` only matters if git is pointed at it.

content.content:
core.hooksPath = .githooks

content.text:
`core.hooksPath = .githooks` and the hook is executable — so future commits on this machine will actually run it with the env exported. Durability is confirmed for the production node.

One residual nuance for completeness: durability depends on `core.hooksPath` being configured locally (it is on the production node). A fresh clone without that git config wouldn't run the hook — but that's a generic hook-installation property, not a gap specific to this fix, and the production capture node is correctly configured. The default-value pattern `${ASSAY_SCRUB_HOSTS:-hc-grc}` also correctly honors an external override while guaranteeing a sane default.

All four verification criteria pass.

input.rationale: P9-PII-1 round 2 fix verified on branch harden/pass-9. All four criteria pass. (1) ZERO leaks: `grep -ri 'hc-grc\.local'` and `grep -ri '[REDACTED]'` over transcripts/ both return 0 — and critically, HEAD (22cbcad) just re-captured the active session plus ~15 transcript files WITH the env set, and none of those re-captured files contain either token, proving the corpus stayed clean through a real capture. (2) Bare 'hc-grc' preserved across 1446 files (matches the ~1400 figure; raw occurrences are higher but the project token is intentionally not over-redacted). (3) The hook (.githooks/pre-commit) does `export ASSAY_SCRUB_HOSTS="${ASSAY_SCRUB_HOSTS:-hc-grc}"` BEFORE invoking scripts/capture_transcripts.py, and honors an externally-set value via the :- default. (4) Both tests pass and discriminate under mutation: removing the export line makes test_precommit_hook_wires_namespace_host_into_capture FAIL (AssertionError at line 225); and I reproduced the exact root-cause mechanism — with ASSAY_SCRUB_HOSTS unset, the scrubber leaves 'hc-proj.local' (the [REDACTED] analog) unredacted, while the test sets the env and asserts the .local form is gone, the bare token preserved, and unrelated settings.local.json untouched. Implementation matches the described contract: _hostname_patterns() redacts the auto-derived machine hostname bare+.local and an ASSAY_SCRUB_HOSTS host's .local form only. Durability confirmed: git core.hooksPath=.githooks and the hook is executable (rwxr-xr-x), so future commits on the production node run it with the env exported. The operator-hostname PII vector is closed and durable against future captures.
concerns: Durability is contingent on git core.hooksPath=.githooks being configured locally — it is on this production node, but a fresh clone without that config would skip the hook and re-introduce [REDACTED]. This is a generic hook-installation property, not specific to this fix, and no repo-side setup script wiring hooksPath was found; acceptable for the dedicated capture node but worth a one-line bootstrap if the workflow ever runs from a fresh clone.

_HOSTNAME_RES is computed once at module import, so the env must be set before the process starts (the hook does this correctly via export); any non-hook caller of the scrubber that sets the env after import would not pick up the rule. Not a leak in the committed corpus, just an invariant to preserve.

content.text: Verified P9-PII-1 round 2 on harden/pass-9: CONFIRMED. The operator-hostname PII vector is now actually closed and durable. Zero genuine leaks of `[REDACTED]` or `[REDACTED]` in transcripts/ (including the ~15 files HEAD just re-captured with the env set), bare `hc-grc` preserved across 1446 files, the hook exports `ASSAY_SCRUB_HOSTS` before capture (honoring external overrides), and both tests pass and discriminate under mutation. `core.hooksPath=.githooks` with an executable hook makes it durable on the production node. Two non-blocking notes recorded re: hooksPath-on-fresh-clone and the import-time env requirement.
